title: 2 Growth Stocks Worth Buying Through the Volatility and Holding for a Lifetime
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: E.l.f. Beauty and Vita Coco are highlighted as durable growth stocks suitable for long-term holding. E.l.f. Beauty has delivered 29 consecutive quarters of double-digit net sales growth and is expanding through strategic brand acquisitions including Hailey Bieber's rhode brand. Vita Coco dominates the U.S. coconut water market with 51-52% share and is experiencing 37% year-over-year sales growth, with significant international expansion opportunities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company demonstrates consistent double-digit growth for 29 consecutive quarters, successful strategic acquisitions (Naturium and rhode), 25% year-over-year net sales growth, and expanding international presence outpacing legacy competitors like L'Oréal and Estée Lauder.

Strong Q1 2026 results with 37% year-over-year sales growth, operating margin expansion from 14.7% to 18.7%, market dominance with 51-52% U.S. coconut water share, minimal debt, $200 million cash position, and significant growth runway as market projected to expand from $5 billion to $19 billion by 2033.
Mentioned only as a reference point for e.l.f. Beauty's original consumer base; no specific analysis or performance data provided.
Characterized as a legacy beauty company struggling with single-digit growth in the same category where e.l.f. Beauty is achieving 25% growth, indicating competitive disadvantage.
Described as a legacy beauty company fighting to maintain single-digit growth, positioned as underperforming compared to e.l.f. Beauty's superior growth trajectory.

title: Vita Coco to Serve as Exclusive Electrolyte Beverage Partner of INTENNSE
author: Not Specified
description: INTENNSE, a co-ed team-based professional tennis league, has partnered with Vita Coco as its exclusive electrolyte beverage partner for the 2026 season. The partnership includes on-site hydration support, product sampling, brand activations, and integration into live-stream and digital experiences. The 2026 season runs from June 5 through August 1, with opening weekend tickets now available.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Vita Coco secured a high-profile partnership with a new professional sports league, providing significant brand exposure through on-site activations, live-stream integration, and dedicated match-night events. This represents a strategic expansion into the sports beverage sponsorship space and aligns with the brand's positioning as a premium electrolyte beverage option.

title: The Market Is Down. These 3 Growth Stocks Are Still Worth Owning Forever.
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: The article highlights three growth stocks worth long-term ownership despite market downturns: e.l.f. Beauty, known for 28 consecutive quarters of sales growth and strong value positioning; Vita Coco, which dominates the coconut water market with a diversified supply chain moat; and Dutch Bros, a coffee chain in early national expansion with proven brand culture and strong loyalty metrics.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 28 consecutive quarters of net sales growth, strong value proposition with products under $15, 38% Q3 growth, expanding to 120 countries from 16, maintains 70% gross margin vs. industry average of 41%, resilient in downturns

Controls 42% of U.S. coconut water market, operates 16 factories across 6 countries creating supply chain moat, coconut water market grew 126% last year, strong demographic tailwind with young health-conscious consumers, early global household penetration with long runway

Opened 154 new stores in 2025, targeting 181+ openings in 2026, aiming for 2,000+ shops by 2029, high engagement metrics in loyalty program, proven model across markets, still only in roughly half the country with significant expansion potential

title: COCO SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Investors Encouraged to Contact Kirby McInerney LLP About Potential Securities Laws Violations
author: Kirby Mcinerney Llp
description: Kirby McInerney LLP is investigating The Vita Coco Company for potential securities law violations following a March 26, 2025 NINGI Research report alleging the company misled investors about growth and operations. The report claims Vita Coco risks losing a Costco contract representing 25% of sales and faces supply chain and operational issues. Vita Coco shares declined 11% on the news.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is under investigation for potential securities law violations based on allegations of misleading investors about growth and operations. The report highlights risks of losing a major customer (Costco, 25% of sales), supply chain issues, and poor performance outside core business, resulting in an 11% stock price decline.

title: The Vita Coco Company Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results
author: Na
description: The Vita Coco Company announced full year 2025 net sales of $610 million, up 18% driven by strong Vita Coco Coconut Water growth of 26%. Net income increased to $71 million and Adjusted EBITDA rose to $98 million. For 2026, the company expects net sales between $680-$700 million and Adjusted EBITDA between $122-$128 million, with growth projected from continued Vita Coco Coconut Water expansion and improved private label trends.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong financial performance with 18% net sales growth, 26% growth in flagship Vita Coco Coconut Water product, net income increase of $15 million, and double-digit EBITDA growth. Positive 2026 guidance with expected sales growth of 11-15% and EBITDA growth of 14-19%. Company maintains strong balance sheet with $197 million cash and no debt. Management expresses confidence in category momentum and brand strength.
